oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Behind the scenes: Fair play, honest competition… and (prayers for) no disease outbreaks
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Dobecka, Caitlin
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: contract
country/state fair
livestock show
oai_dc:dc.dc:description:
What brings people together better than animals? Livestock events bring together animals, exhibitors and the public in a uniquely engaging environment. Along with that environment comes the need for public health, biosecurity and rules – enter, you, the veterinarian, tasked with the critical roles of animal health and welfare, public health, food safety and fair competi­tion. Veterinarians are often approached to serve as the veteri­narian on record for such events, yet few have formal training in navigating the medical, regulatory and ethical responsibili­ties that come with the role. This Clinical Skills presentation will provide framework for veterinarians to fulfill a veterinary services contract for any livestock or equine event. Necessary topics to consider when taking on a veterinary ser­vices contract for any event involving animals include:
developing clear event expectations for scope of services, compensation, legal obligations establishing clear communication channels within and outside of event animal health inspections biosecurity protocols animal health and welfare protocols ensuring compliance with exhibition rules and state regulations navigating cases with professional integrity
By lifting the curtain on the behind-the-scenes responsibilities, this session equips veterinarians with confidence, plans, and perspective to protect animal health at livestock events.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 123-126
